req.title: Card PIN change (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two orthogonal behaviours: (1) a rate-limit of 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window and (2) idempotency via Idempotency-Key. Test design covers: EP – a single valid PIN change (normal partition); BVA – exactly 5 changes (upper boundary, allowed) and the 6th change (upper boundary + 1, rejected); NEG – replay of a duplicate Idempotency-Key returning the cached response; STC – transition from 'limit not reached' through 'limit reached' to '429 blocked'. Each test resets state via a unique card fixture and a helper that clears the rate-limit counter, ensuring determinism and idempotency of the test suite itself.

tests.name: test_single_pin_change_returns_200
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token is available via <fixture: auth_token>
A card owned by the caller is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_card>
Rate-limit counter for the card is reset to 0 via <helper_fn: reset_rate_limit(card_id)>
actions: PATCH /cards/{card_id}/pin with a valid new PIN and a unique Idempotency-Key header
expected: Response body (if 200) contains no error fields
Subsequent authenticated PIN verification succeeds with the new PIN
trace: EP: valid input within rate-limit window maps to success partition

tests.name: test_sixth_pin_change_returns_429
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token is available via <fixture: auth_token>
A card owned by the caller is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_card>
Rate-limit counter for the card is reset to 0 via <helper_fn: reset_rate_limit(card_id)>
Five prior PIN changes have been successfully submitted within the same 24-hour window
actions: PATCH /cards/{card_id}/pin five times with distinct Idempotency-Keys (exhaust the limit)
Submit a sixth PATCH /cards/{card_id}/pin with a new distinct Idempotency-Key
expected: First five requests each return HTTP 200 or 204
Response body of 429 includes a rate-limit error indicator
trace: R-FIN-097: 429 when rate limit exceeded (error pathway)
NEG: exercising the rejection partition beyond the allowed quota

tests.name: test_exactly_five_pin_changes_all_succeed
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token is available via <fixture: auth_token>
A card owned by the caller is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_card>
Rate-limit counter for the card is reset to 0 via <helper_fn: reset_rate_limit(card_id)>
actions: Submit exactly 5 PATCH /cards/{card_id}/pin requests with distinct Idempotency-Keys within the 24-hour window
expected: All five requests return HTTP 200 or 204
No 429 is returned for any of the five requests
trace: R-FIN-097: at most 5 changes per 24-hour window
BVA: upper boundary value N=5 must be accepted; N+1=6 must be rejected (see companion NEG test)

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token is available via <fixture: auth_token>
A card owned by the caller is provisioned via <fixture: fresh_card>
Rate-limit counter for the card is reset to 0 via <helper_fn: reset_rate_limit(card_id)>
actions: Submit PATCH /cards/{card_id}/pin with a unique Idempotency-Key and record the response
Replay the identical request (same Idempotency-Key and body) a second time
expected: Second (duplicate) request returns the same HTTP status code as the first
Rate-limit counter increments by 1, not 2, confirming the replay is not double-counted
trace: R-FIN-097: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
STC: state transition from 'pending' -> 'completed' should not re-enter 'processing' on replay
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, reset_rate_limit, get_rate_limit_count

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(auth_token, fresh_card):
    card_id = fresh_card["id"]
    reset_rate_limit(card_id, auth_token)
    client = api_client()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    payload = {"new_pin": "5678"}
    first = client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 204), f"First request failed: {first.status_code}"
    second = client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == first.status_code, (
        f"Idempotency broken: 1st={first.status_code}, 2nd={second.status_code}"
    )
    if first.status_code == 200:
        assert second.json() == first.json(), "Response bodies differ on replay"
    count = get_rate_limit_count(card_id, auth_token)
    assert count == 1, f"Expected rate-limit count=1, got {count}"

tests.name: test_pin_change_rejected_for_card_not_owned_by_caller
preconditions: Authenticated customer session token is available via <fixture: auth_token>
A card belonging to a DIFFERENT customer is provisioned via <fixture: foreign_card>
Rate-limit counter is irrelevant (precondition violation should short-circuit)
actions: PATCH /cards/{foreign_card_id}/pin with the caller's auth token and a valid payload
expected: HTTP 403 or 404 response (card does not belong to caller)
Response body contains an authorization or ownership error
No PIN change is effected on the foreign card
trace: DT: decision column where ownership=false forces rejection regardless of other conditions
